title: Dialect Portability, NoSQL Tradeoffs, Distribution, and Production Defense · Data Contract Brief: Production Observability
summary: A team must move a browser prototype toward PostgreSQL, justify any non-relational component, and defend the complete data system. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql production observability increment in a production-ready cross-dialect data system portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define correctness, latency, saturation, lock, plan-regression, backup, and data-quality signals with response thresholds.
Explain replication, partitioning, distributed transactions, consistency choices, and failure boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Predict Production Observability · Theory Production Observability
steps.instruction:
theory case — Move a SQLite 3.49.1 theory artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 4; justify any non-relational component.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Production Observability contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Production Observability · Theory Production Observability.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define correctness, latency, saturation, lock, plan-regression, backup, and data-quality signals with response thresholds. For Predict Production Observability · Theory Production Observability, keep Production Observability connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: Define correctness, latency, saturation, lock, plan-regression, backup, and data-quality signals with response thresholds. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Move a SQLite 3.49.1 worked example artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 4; justify any non-relational component. A defensible solution applies Production Observability, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a production-ready cross-dialect data system portfolio.

content.code:
SELECT ticket_id, status, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE status <> 'resolved'
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
-- Record SQLite behavior, then verify syntax and semantics against PostgreSQL 18.4.
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Database monitoring is complete when CPU and disk are graphed. This fails the Production Observability contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Production Observability evidence · Theory Production Observability
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-production-observability-1b4siv" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; dialect, storage-model, distribution, or production evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Production Observability evidence · Theory Production Observability.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Production Observability into observable performance evidence. For Build Production Observability evidence · Theory Production Observability, keep Production Observability connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-production-observability-1b4siv
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN
SELECT team_id, COUNT(*) AS backlog
FROM tickets WHERE status <> 'resolved'
GROUP BY team_id ORDER BY team_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
